title: HealthLog – Overview
description: Daily health snapshot: last night's sleep, HRV & resting heart rate, stress score and Body Battery, sleep trends, overnight vitals, activity, body weight, and the nightly analysis' findings. Stress and Body Battery are derived proxies calibrated to your own baseline, not Garmin-comparable values. Bedtime/wake-up clock hours are computed via the hidden 'tz' constant (default Europe/Vienna); other times use the dashboard timezone (Settings → Timezone, default Europe/Vienna). Time window: 30 days.

panels.title: Last Night – Total Sleep
panels.description: Total sleep duration for the most recent night. Yellow ≥ 6 h, green ≥ 7 h (WHO recommendation).

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, total_sleep_h AS "Total Sleep" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Last Night – Sleep Efficiency
panels.description: % of time in bed actually asleep. Calculated from in-bed timestamps when available, otherwise total_sleep / (sleep + awake). Yellow ≥ 75 %, green ≥ 85 %.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(efficiency_pct::numeric, 0) AS "Sleep Efficiency" FROM sleep_metrics WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Today – HRV
panels.description: Latest daily HRV average (ms). Higher = better autonomic recovery. Yellow ≥ 30 ms, green ≥ 50 ms.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, avg AS "HRV" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'heart_rate_variability' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Today – Resting HR
panels.description: Latest daily resting heart rate minimum (bpm). Lower = better cardiovascular fitness. Yellow ≥ 65 bpm, red ≥ 75 bpm.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, vmin AS "Resting HR" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'resting_heart_rate' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Stress Score — latest day
panels.description: Overall daily stress (0-100), most recent day in range. A derived proxy from the heart-rate elevation above your resting baseline (workouts excluded), HRV-calibrated — NOT a Garmin value. Blue rest < 25, green low < 50, orange medium < 75, red high.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, score AS "Stress Score" FROM stress_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Body Battery — current reserve
panels.description: Latest energy reserve (0-100) in range: the stress timeline integrated against recovery — stress and workouts drain it, calm rest and sleep charge it. A proxy on the stress proxy (no RR intervals), relative to your own baseline, NOT a Garmin value. Red near-empty < 20, orange < 50, green charged.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ts AS "time", level AS "Body Battery" FROM body_battery_intraday WHERE $__timeFilter(ts) AND level IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ts

panels.title: Avg Total Sleep (7d)
panels.description: Average total sleep duration over the last 7 nights. Yellow ≥ 6 h, green ≥ 7 h.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(total_sleep_h)::numeric, 1) AS "Avg Total Sleep" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE sleep_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.title: Avg Deep Sleep (7d)
panels.description: Average deep (slow-wave) sleep over the last 7 nights. Yellow ≥ 0.8 h, green ≥ 1.2 h.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(deep_h)::numeric, 1) AS "Avg Deep Sleep" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE sleep_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.title: Avg REM Sleep (7d)
panels.description: Average REM sleep over the last 7 nights. Yellow ≥ 1.0 h, green ≥ 1.5 h.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(rem_h)::numeric, 1) AS "Avg REM Sleep" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE sleep_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.title: Avg Sleep Efficiency (7d)
panels.description: Average sleep efficiency over the last 7 nights. Calculated from in-bed timestamps when available, otherwise total_sleep / (sleep + awake). Yellow ≥ 75 %, green ≥ 85 %.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(efficiency_pct)::numeric, 0) AS "Avg Sleep Efficiency" FROM sleep_metrics WHERE sleep_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.title: Sleep Phases
panels.description: Stacked hours per phase for each night (Deep + Core + REM + Awake). Total bar height is time asleep plus awake-in-bed; the Awake segment on top shows fragmentation. Hover for the per-phase breakdown.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, deep_h AS "Deep", core_h AS "Core", rem_h AS "REM", awake_h AS "Awake" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Sleep Duration
panels.description: Total sleep per night. Dashed line is the 7-day moving average.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, total_sleep_h AS "Total Sleep (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(AVG(total_sleep_h) OVER (ORDER BY sleep_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 2) AS "7d Trend (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Sleep Efficiency
panels.description: % of time in bed actually asleep. Calculated from in-bed timestamps when available, otherwise total_sleep / (sleep + awake). Dashed line is the 7-day moving average.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, efficiency_pct AS "Sleep Efficiency (%)" FROM sleep_metrics WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(AVG(efficiency_pct) OVER (ORDER BY sleep_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 1) AS "7d Trend (%)" FROM sleep_metrics WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Bedtime
panels.description: Hour of sleep onset on a 20–30 scale (20 = 20:00, 24 = midnight, 26 = 02:00 next day). Uses the selected Timezone. Dashed line is the 7-day moving average.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz'))/60.0 + CASE WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) < 12 THEN 24 ELSE 0 END AS "Bedtime (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) AND sleep_start IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sleep_date

SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz'))/60.0 + CASE WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_start AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) < 12 THEN 24 ELSE 0 END) OVER (ORDER BY sleep_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 2) AS "7d Trend (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) AND sleep_start IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Wake-up Time
panels.description: Hour of wake-up in the selected Timezone. Dashed line is the 7-day moving average.

targets.rawSql: SELECT sleep_date AS time, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_end AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (sleep_end AT TIME ZONE '$tz'))/60.0 AS "Wake-up (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) AND sleep_end IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sleep_date

SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (sleep_end AT TIME ZONE '$tz')) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (sleep_end AT TIME ZONE '$tz'))/60.0) OVER (ORDER BY sleep_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 2) AS "7d Trend (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) AND sleep_end IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Respiratory Rate & Wrist Temperature
panels.description: Overnight recovery markers. Respiratory rate (left axis, breaths/min): a stable baseline is normal; sustained elevation can precede illness or follow hard training. Wrist temperature (right axis, °C) is shown as the deviation around your own baseline, not an absolute body temperature. Dashed lines are 7-day moving averages.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, avg AS "Resp Rate (/min)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'respiratory_rate' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day
SELECT day AS time, ROUND(avg::numeric, 2) AS "Wrist Temp (°C)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'apple_sleeping_wrist_temperature' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

SELECT day AS time, ROUND(AVG(avg) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 1) AS "Resp Rate Trend (/min)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'respiratory_rate' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

SELECT day AS time, ROUND(AVG(avg) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 2) AS "Wrist Temp Trend (°C)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'apple_sleeping_wrist_temperature' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Blood Oxygen Saturation (SpO₂)
panels.description: Overnight blood oxygen saturation (%). Typical healthy range is ~95–100 %; brief dips can occur normally. Persistently low overnight values may warrant attention. Dashed line is the 7-day moving average.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, ROUND(avg::numeric, 1) AS "SpO₂ (%)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'blood_oxygen_saturation' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

SELECT day AS time, ROUND(AVG(avg) OVER (ORDER BY day ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 1) AS "7d Trend (%)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'blood_oxygen_saturation' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Breathing Disturbances (count / night)
panels.description: Nightly breathing-disturbance count from the Apple Watch (the sleep-apnoea indicator, watchOS 11+). Shows data only when the watch records it and HAE exports the breathing_disturbances metric.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, sum AS "Disturbances" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'breathing_disturbances' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Time in stress zones (per day)
panels.description: Minutes per day in each stress zone, plus workout (Active) minutes. Stacked bars.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, rest_min AS "Rest", low_min AS "Low", medium_min AS "Medium", high_min AS "High", active_min AS "Active" FROM stress_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Daily stress score — trend
panels.description: Overall daily stress score over time. Interpret relative to your own baseline, not Garmin.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, score AS "Stress Score" FROM stress_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Intraday stress timeline
panels.description: Per-bucket stress (0-100) over the selected range, coloured by zone. Gaps are unmeasured (watch off / workout). For a usable timeline set Health Auto Export's Time Grouping to Minute.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ts AS "time", stress AS "Stress" FROM stress_intraday WHERE $__timeFilter(ts) AND stress IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ts

panels.title: Intraday Body Battery timeline
panels.description: Per-bucket energy reserve (0-100) over the selected range. Rises during calm rest and sleep, falls with stress and workouts. For a usable timeline set Health Auto Export's Time Grouping to Minute.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ts AS "time", level AS "Body Battery" FROM body_battery_intraday WHERE $__timeFilter(ts) AND level IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ts

panels.title: Body Battery — daily wake / high / low
panels.description: Per day: the level you woke with (end of main sleep), the day's peak and trough. Wake and high near 100 = well recharged; a low trough = the tank ran empty.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, wake_level AS "Wake", high_level AS "High", low_level AS "Low" FROM body_battery_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Charged vs drained (per day)
panels.description: Total points the battery gained (charged) and lost (drained) each day. Charged should outweigh drained on a well-recovered day.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, charged AS "Charged", drained AS "Drained" FROM body_battery_daily WHERE $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Daily Steps
panels.description: Total step count per day (sum of all intraday samples). A common general-health target is ~10,000 steps/day.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, sum AS "Steps" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'step_count' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Active Energy
panels.description: Active energy burned per day (kcal) — movement on top of basal metabolism. Sum of all intraday samples.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, sum AS "Active Energy (kcal)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'active_energy' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Exercise Minutes
panels.description: Apple exercise-ring minutes per day (apple_exercise_time, sum of all intraday samples). The Apple default goal is 30 min/day.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, sum AS "Exercise (min)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'apple_exercise_time' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Walking / Running Distance (km)
panels.description: Daily walking + running distance (walking_running_distance, sum of all intraday samples) — everyday movement, not just workouts.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, ROUND(sum::numeric, 1) AS "Distance (km)" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'walking_running_distance' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Latest Weight
panels.description: Most recent body-mass reading within the selected time range (daily average when the scale reports several).

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, ROUND(avg::numeric, 1) AS "Weight" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'weight_body_mass' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

panels.title: Weight Trend
panels.description: Daily body mass (average of the day's readings). Sparse by nature — points appear only on days with a measurement.

targets.rawSql: SELECT day AS time, ROUND(avg::numeric, 1) AS "Weight" FROM daily_metrics WHERE metric = 'weight_body_mass' AND $__timeFilter(day::timestamptz) ORDER BY day

SELECT sleep_date AS time, ROUND(AVG(total_sleep_h) OVER (ORDER BY sleep_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)::numeric, 2) AS "7d Trend (h)" FROM sleep_nightly WHERE $__timeFilter(sleep_date::timestamptz) ORDER BY sleep_date

panels.title: Recent Findings
panels.description: Latest findings of every kind from the nightly analysis (anomalies, recovery alerts, training load, consistency, ...). Correlations are excluded here - they have their own panel below.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TO_CHAR(day, 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS "Date", kind AS "Kind", metric_a AS "Metric", detail AS "Detail", note AS "Note" FROM findings_feed WHERE kind <> 'correlation' ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Top Correlations
panels.description: Lagged Spearman relationships between metrics, ranked by report_priority (analysis.py): cross-domain links (e.g. training load → next-day respiratory rate) rank above expected within-subsystem pairs (total vs deep sleep, average vs resting HR). The Priority column reflects the stored details.priority_tier; r is the de-trended coefficient, lag the strongest lead/lag.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(ra.display_name, f.metric_a) || ' → ' || COALESCE(rb.display_name, f.metric_b) AS "Relationship", CASE WHEN COALESCE(f.lag_days,0) = 0 THEN 'same day' ELSE f.lag_days::text || 'd lag' END AS "Lag", ROUND(f.coefficient::numeric, 2) AS "r", CASE COALESCE((f.details->>'priority_tier')::int, 1) WHEN 2 THEN 'cross-domain' WHEN 0 THEN 'expected' ELSE 'neutral' END AS "Priority" FROM findings f LEFT JOIN metric_registry ra ON ra.metric = f.metric_a LEFT JOIN metric_registry rb ON rb.metric = f.metric_b WHERE f.kind = 'correlation' ORDER BY COALESCE((f.details->>'priority_tier')::int, 1) DESC, ABS(f.coefficient) DESC LIMIT 30

list.name: tz
list.label: Timezone
list.description: Hidden constant. Timezone name used only for the SQL-side local clock-hour math of the bedtime/wake-up panels (EXTRACT(HOUR FROM sleep_start AT TIME ZONE ...)), which Grafana's dashboard timezone cannot reach. Change the value here to re-localise those computations.
